name: Prisma ORM
what_it_does: Prisma is a TypeScript-first ORM that generates a type-safe database client from a declarative schema file, with built-in migration tooling and support for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, MongoDB, CockroachDB, and SQL Server.
use_cases: Agents performing type-safe CRUD operations against relational databases without raw SQL
Automated schema migrations during CI/CD pipelines or agent-driven deployments
Querying relational data with nested include/select to minimize round trips in agent workflows
Seeding databases with structured data during environment setup by agent scripts
Introspecting an existing database schema to generate a Prisma schema for a new agent integration
not_for: Raw SQL performance tuning where full query control is required — Prisma's query engine adds overhead
Very large monorepos with dozens of microservices sharing one schema — schema coupling becomes a liability
Environments that cannot run Node.js or the Rust-based Prisma query engine binary
best_when: You are building a TypeScript/Node.js backend and want end-to-end type safety from database to application layer with minimal boilerplate.
avoid_when: You need maximum raw SQL performance, work in a non-Node environment, or your team strongly prefers SQL-first query composition over generated clients.

auth.notes: Library — no auth required for Prisma itself. Database connection credentials are passed via DATABASE_URL environment variable. Prisma Data Platform (cloud) uses its own auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully free OSS library (Apache 2.0). Prisma Accelerate (connection pooling/caching) and Prisma Pulse (change data capture) are paid add-ons.
pricing.paid_tiers: Prisma Accelerate: free tier up to 60,000 queries/month; paid from $19/month. Prisma Pulse: contact for pricing.
pricing.notes: Core ORM is free forever. Cloud add-ons are optional. Self-hosted deployments incur no Prisma costs.

performance.rate_limits: None — client library. Database connection pool size configurable.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Prisma throws typed PrismaClientKnownRequestError with error codes (P2002 for unique constraint, P2025 for record not found, etc.) — highly structured and catchable by agents.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No built-in idempotency keys; agents must implement upsert() or findFirst-then-create patterns for safe retries.
known_agent_gotchas: Prisma Client must be regenerated (prisma generate) after any schema change — stale client causes runtime type errors that are hard to diagnose
N+1 query problem is easy to introduce when agents loop over results and call related queries — always use include/select for nested data
Long-running agent transactions can exhaust the connection pool — default pool size is low (5-10) and must be tuned for concurrent agents
prisma migrate deploy (production) vs prisma migrate dev (development) behave differently — using dev in CI can wipe shadow databases unexpectedly
MongoDB support uses a separate connector with limited transaction support and no full-text search — many Prisma features don't apply to MongoDB

security_score_components.security_notes: DATABASE_URL must be kept secret; Prisma does not enforce TLS but most database providers do. Parameterized queries prevent SQL injection by default. Raw query escape hatches (prisma.$queryRaw) require manual sanitization.
